proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.1pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 remains under D-8 review per project_anchor §7 (Day-6 forced-exit dominance); monitor for R-multiple drift on next archive refresh
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.1pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.1pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio has 0 Bear exposure and no regime concentration triggered (0.7647 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-15) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +33.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Portfolio regime concentration observed 0.7647 is approaching the 0.80 trigger threshold; if Bear re-emerges, boost-driven entries should be monitored to avoid tipping the concentration trigger.
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +33.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +33.3pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline of 54.9%) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio has 0 Bear exposure and no regime concentration is triggered (observed 0.7647 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-15) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (94.1%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI until either (a) signal mix diversifies below 85% via natural Day-6 exits, or (b) Bull×UP_TRI / Choppy×UP_TRI cohorts graduate above Candidate tier. Prefer any new adds to be non-UP_TRI (BULL_PROXY, DN_TRI, or BEAR_PROXY) to rebalance signal exposure.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: If Bull regime rolls to Bear or Choppy tightens, 16 correlated UP_TRI longs unwind together with no validated edge to justify the concentration — book-wide drawdown from a single directional/signal factor rather than idiosyncratic name risk.

evidence: Choppy regime share = 4/17 = 23.53% — mixed/unstable regime backdrop
All four concentrated cells (UP_TRI×Choppy×Metal, UP_TRI×Bull×Infra, UP_TRI×Bull×CapGoods, UP_TRI×Bull×Chem) match only Candidate-tier cohorts
Bull×UP_TRI cohort n=59 (Candidate); Choppy×UP_TRI cohort n=298 (Candidate) — no validated edge
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI until either (a) signal mix diversifies below 85% via natural Day-6 exits, or (b) Bull×UP_TRI / Choppy×UP_TRI cohorts graduate above Candidate tier. Prefer any new adds to be non-UP_TRI (BULL_PROXY, DN_TRI, or BEAR_PROXY) to rebalance signal exposure.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: If Bull regime rolls to Bear or Choppy tightens, 16 correlated UP_TRI longs unwind together with no validated edge to justify the concentration — book-wide drawdown from a single directional/signal factor rather than idiosyncratic name risk.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI dominates the signal mix at 16/17 = 94.1% (well above the 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long. Matched Bull×UP_TRI and Choppy×UP_TRI cohorts sit only at Candidate tier (n=59 and n=298) — unvalidated edge. Regime concentration (Bull 76.5%) and individual cells (max 11.76%) are within tolerance, but the signal-type monoculture on unvalidated cohorts is the actionable risk.
